---
title: "Frequentist Poisson Model — GLMM"
author: "Astrid Elmann Hansen"
date: "2026-05-20"
output:
  html_document:
    output_dir: "../html_output"
---

# Setup

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, lme4, parameters)

theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```

# Load data

```{r}
df <- read_csv('../data/round.csv')
```

# Inspect data

```{r}
glimpse(df)
```

# Convert variables

```{r}
df <- df %>%
  arrange(player_id, round) %>%
  group_by(player_id, condition) %>%
  mutate(encounter = row_number()) %>%          # 1st vs 2nd time player saw this condition
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    player_id     = factor(player_id),
    group         = factor(group),
    condition_FUN = as.integer(condition == "FUN"),   # numeric 0/1 for GGLM
    round_c       = round - mean(round),              # centred round position
    encounter     = factor(encounter)
  ) %>%
  arrange(player_id)
```


# Model

```{r}
# Primary model: matches the main brms model
m1_glmm <- glmer(
  tags_in_round ~ condition_FUN + (1 | group) + (1 | player_id),
  data    = df,
  family  = poisson(link = "log")
)

# Sensitivity: matches the brms + encounter model
m2_glmm <- glmer(
  tags_in_round ~ condition_FUN + encounter + (1 | group) + (1 | player_id),
  data    = df,
  family  = poisson(link = "log")
)

# IRRs with Wald CIs (fast)
parameters::model_parameters(m1_glmm, exponentiate = TRUE)
parameters::model_parameters(m2_glmm, exponentiate = TRUE)

# Profile-likelihood CIs (slower but better for small samples)
exp(confint(m1_glmm, parm = "condition_FUN", method = "profile"))
```
